name: semiotic
description: React data visualization library for charts, networks, and beyond

function.name: LineChart
function.description: Line traces with curve interpolation, area fill, and point markers. Use for time series, trends, and continuous data.

xAccessor.description: Key or accessor function for x-axis values

yAccessor.description: Key or accessor function for y-axis values

lineBy.description: Key to group data into separate lines

lineDataAccessor.description: Key for the coordinates array within each line object

colorBy.description: Key to determine color encoding per line/group

colorScheme.description: Named d3 color scheme or array of color strings

showPoints.description: Show data point markers on the line

pointRadius.description: Radius of point markers when showPoints is true

fillArea.description: Fill the area under the line

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

function.name: AreaChart
function.description: Filled area chart with optional stroke line. Use for showing volume or magnitude over time.

areaBy.description: Key to group data into separate areas

lineDataAccessor.description: Key for the coordinates array within each area object

colorScheme.description: Named d3 color scheme or array of color strings

showLine.description: Show stroke line on top of area

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

function.description: Individual data points plotted by x/y position with optional size and color encoding.

function.name: QuadrantChart
function.description: Scatterplot divided into four labeled, colored quadrants by center lines. Use for BCG matrices, priority matrices, and any 2x2 strategic framework.

xCenter.description: X-coordinate of the vertical center line. Defaults to midpoint of x domain.

yCenter.description: Y-coordinate of the horizontal center line. Defaults to midpoint of y domain.

quadrants.description: Configuration for the four quadrants: { topRight, topLeft, bottomRight, bottomLeft }, each with { label, color, opacity? }

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

function.name: MultiAxisLineChart
function.description: Dual Y-axis line chart for comparing two series with different scales on the same x axis. Data is unitized (normalized to [0,1]) internally; left axis shows series[0] values and right axis shows series[1] values in original units. Falls back to standard multi-line if not exactly 2 series.

data.description: Array of data objects shared by both series

series.description: Exactly 2 series configs for dual-axis mode. Each: { yAccessor, label?, color?, format?, extent? }

yAccessor.description: Field name for y values of this series

extent.description: Fixed [min, max] extent. Required for push API streaming.

function.name: BubbleChart
function.description: Scatterplot with required size dimension for three-variable comparison. Bubble area encodes a numeric value.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

function.name: ConnectedScatterplot
function.description: Scatterplot where points are connected in order, showing trajectories through 2D space. Viridis-colored start→end, white halo under lines.

orderLabel.description: Label for the ordering metric in tooltips

pointIdAccessor.description: Accessor for unique point IDs, used by point-anchored annotations

function.description: Stacked bars for part-to-whole comparisons across categories. Requires stackBy to define the stacking dimension.

stackBy.description: Key to define the stacking dimension (required)

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). function.name: LikertChart
function.description: Visualize Likert scale survey responses. Horizontal (default): diverging bar chart centered at 0% — negative levels extend left, positive right, neutral (if odd count) split 50/50 across centerline. Vertical: stacked 100% bar chart. Supports raw integer scores (1-based, aggregated automatically) or pre-aggregated (question, level, count) data. The levels array defines polarity: first half = negative, second half = positive, center = neutral (if odd). Works with any scale size (3-point to 7-point+). Supports push API for streaming — accumulates raw data and re-aggregates on each push.

data.description: Array of raw response or pre-aggregated data objects

levels.description: Ordered response labels, most negative to most positive (required). Odd count = center is neutral.

valueAccessor.description: Integer score field for raw response mode (1-based: score 1 → levels[0])

levelAccessor.description: Level name field for pre-aggregated mode. Each value must match an entry in levels.

colorScheme.description: One color per level. Use a diverging palette (red → gray → blue). Defaults to Carbon-inspired diverging.

function.name: GroupedBarChart
function.description: Side-by-side bars for comparing sub-categories within categories. Requires groupBy to define grouping.

groupBy.description: Key to define the grouping dimension (required)

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

childrenAccessor.description: Key for the children array in each node

colorByDepth.description: Color nodes by their depth in the hierarchy

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

function.name: OrbitDiagram
function.description: Animated orbital diagram showing hierarchical data as nodes orbiting a center. Supports flat, solar, atomic, and custom ring arrangements.

data.description: Hierarchical root object with children: { name: 'root', children: [...] }

childrenAccessor.description: Key to access children from each datum

colorBy.description: Field or function for node color

speed.description: Orbit speed in degrees per frame

eccentricity.description: Vertical squash for elliptical orbits (1 = circle)

function.name: FunnelChart
function.description: Funnel visualization with two orientations. Horizontal (default): steps top-to-bottom with centered bars and trapezoid connectors; multi-category mirrors around center axis. Vertical: steps on x-axis as vertical bars with hatched dropoff stacking (solid = retained, hatched = dropoff from previous step); multi-category renders grouped bars.

data.description: Array of data objects with step and value fields

valueAccessor.description: Key for numeric value per step

categoryAccessor.description: Key to split each step into mirrored categories (optional)

colorBy.description: Key to determine color encoding per category

colorScheme.description: Named d3 color scheme or array of color strings

orientation.description: Horizontal (default): centered bars top-to-bottom with trapezoid connectors. Vertical: vertical bars with hatched dropoff stacking — solid = retained, hatched = dropoff from previous step. Multi-category renders grouped bars in vertical mode.

connectorOpacity.description: Opacity of trapezoid connectors between steps (0-1). Horizontal orientation only.

showCategoryTicks.description: Show category tick labels on ordinal axis

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

margin.description: Chart margins: { top, right, bottom, left }

tooltip.description: Tooltip content function or config. Default shows step name, value, and percentage of first step.

function.name: SwimlaneChart
function.description: Categorical lanes with sequentially stacked items colored by subcategory. Unlike StackedBarChart, the same subcategory can appear multiple times in the same lane — items stack left-to-right (horizontal) or bottom-to-top (vertical) in data order. Supports brush for value-axis selection and push API for streaming.

data.description: Array of data objects. Omit for push API mode.

subcategoryAccessor.description: Key for item subcategory (color grouping within lanes). Required. Duplicate subcategories in the same lane stack sequentially.

valueAccessor.description: Key for item size/duration along the value axis

colorBy.description: Key to determine color encoding. Defaults to subcategoryAccessor.

colorScheme.description: Named d3 color scheme or array of color strings

orientation.description: Horizontal renders lanes as rows; vertical as columns.

onBrush.description: Callback with { r: [min, max] } or null when brush clears

showCategoryTicks.description: Show lane labels on the category axis

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart.

margin.description: Chart margins: { top, right, bottom, left }
